flow_points: Canada started strongly, scoring 50 runs for 0 wickets in the Powerplay.
New Zealand responded aggressively, scoring 60 runs for 2 wickets in their Powerplay (RR: 10.0).
YS Samra anchored Canada's innings with 110(67), contributing 63.6% of their total runs.
Canada's Jaskaran Singh was expensive with an economy of 14.31, significantly above his career average.
Shivam Sharma also struggled with an economy of 11.5, well above his career average.
Ansh Patel had a highly expensive outing with an economy of 12.0, far exceeding his career average.
GD Phillips anchored New Zealand's chase, scoring 76(41) and contributing 43.2% of the team's runs.
JA Duffy sustained defensive pressure for New Zealand, registering an impact score of 59.78.
New Zealand's high-scoring finish in the Death Overs (RR: 42.0) decisively shifted match momentum.
New Zealand won the match by 8 wickets.
